key_insight: MOSAIC enables continual learning when new gait sensor modalities arrive over time by stabilizing newly learned modality features, separating sensor-specific statistics, and recovering plasticity without catastrophic forgetting.
engineering_relevance: This is relevant for clinical and wearable sensing systems that must adapt to upgraded devices, changing protocols, or multi-center deployments without retraining from scratch or retaining all historical data.
method: Modality-Specific Warm-Up to reduce unreliable cross-modal distillation from a “toxic teacher” early in learning.
Statistics-decoupled MSBN to isolate sensor-specific distributions while keeping a shared semantic backbone.
Curriculum-guided repulsive objective for plasticity recovery to preserve legacy knowledge and regain modality-specific capacity.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation on three multimodal Parkinson’s gait datasets, where MOSAIC improves final performance and reduces forgetting.
watch_item: The approach adds architectural and training complexity, so its robustness depends on careful tuning when modalities, data quality, or dataset shift differ from the benchmark setting.
paper_title: MOSAIC: Modality-Specific Adaptation for Incremental Continual Learning in Parkinson's Disease Gait Assessment
